steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The question asks what the two authors mainly disagree about. Text 1 concludes Ollantay was created in the late 1700s based on its Spanish writing style. Text 2 argues it is an authentic Inca work from the 1500s based on structural and cultural details. The core dispute is the play's origin date.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'What inspired a Spanish writer in the 1700s to write a play about the Inca Empire': Neither text discusses a Spanish writer or what inspired one — this misreads both texts entirely. ✗
  • Choice B — 'Whether Ollantay is an Inca play from the 1500s or was instead created much later, in the 1700s': This is exactly the dispute — Text 1 says 1700s creation, Text 2 says 1500s Inca origin. ✓
  • Choice C — 'How the Inca author of Ollantay knew so much about plays from Spain': Text 2 does not claim an Inca author knew about Spanish plays; it says Spanish influence crept in later during transcription. Neither text debates this question. ✗
  • Choice D — 'Why Inca plays became more popular in the 1700s than they had been when they were first performed in the 1500s': Neither text discusses the relative popularity of Inca plays across centuries. ✗
That matches choice B — B is correct.
